programme.desc: A bunch of balloons carry Daisy, Pluto and Minnie away in the sky.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Minnie's Masquerade
programme.desc: The clubhouse pals help Minnie get ready for her Masquerade Ball before donning their favourite costumes for Professor Von Drake's big Clubhouse Costume Show.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Mickey's Camp Out
programme.desc: In order to earn Clubhouse Camp Out Badges Mickey and his pals pair up with a buddy.

programme.title: The Rocketeer: Pack-a-Doodle / Sitti's Visit
programme.desc: Rolland invents the Pack-a-Doodle to help him earn his camping badge while on the newest Trailblazer Troop adventure. Sitti visits for the Hughesville Plate lunch food festival.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Double Doggie Dare!/ Empire State of Mind
programme.desc: When a trip to the Empire State Building is cancelled, Bob signs up for a game show; The pugs want to bring the Empire State Building to Bob.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Welcome to Puppy Playcare!/ The Naptime Chronicles
programme.desc: When Bob's finishing touch for Puppy Playcare breaks, Bingo and Rolly make it their mission to find a replacement before Playcare opens! The Pugs help Hissy nap while at Playcare!

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Keia's Birthday Balloon Bash / Hide-and-Go-Sleep
programme.desc: Bingo and Rolly go on a mission to find a special balloon for Keia's birthday party. When the pugs' cousin, Cody, goes missing during a game of hide-and-seek, Bingo and Rolly must find him before his family comes to pick him up.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Mixed-Up Adventures: Goofasaur!/ Teahouse Helpers
programme.desc: A tour of Tokyo goes sideways when Goofy accidentally unleashes a robot dinosaur upon the city! Minnie takes her friends to experience a traditional Japanese tea ceremony.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Mixed-Up Adventures: Mickey's New Mouse House/Millie and Melody's Sleepover!
programme.desc: Mickey and his pals finish building Mickey's brand new house! Millie and Melody have a birthday sleepover at Minnie and Daisy's vacation house!

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Bob's Blue Belt/ The Playcare Heist
programme.desc: Bob's blue belt doesn't arrive in time for his martial arts class photo, so Bingo and Rolly make it their mission to get him a belt. When Keia realizes she accidentally left her favorite stuffed animal at Playcare, she and the pugs set off to rescue it.

programme.title: Muppet Babies: Animal and the Magic Mummy / The Friends of Zorna Club
programme.desc: Follow the Muppet Babies on various adventures as they let their imaginations take flight.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: Bringing This Family Home/ Flight of the Penguin
programme.desc: Pip and Freddy help fly KC's family home to meet their new baby cousin! The Junior Fliers try to deliver two babies at once.

programme.title: Mira, Royal Detective: The Case of the Chiseling Chiselers / A Patchwork Mystery
programme.desc: When the royal sword goes missing, Mira agrees to help Veer find it. Then, when a tapestry that Priya created for Jalpur's anniversary celebration winds up nowhere to be found, Mira sets out on the case to find it before the party begins.

programme.title: Disney Junior Ready for Preschool: Letters
programme.desc: The friends show off their favourite letters.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: The Purrfect Little Helper/ The Colorful Chameleon
programme.desc: When Pip and Freddy have to deliver a kitten, they think it's a mistake since there are no cats at T.O.T.S. The Junior Fliers must deliver a baby chameleon that keeps disappearing!

programme.title: Bluey: Hammerbarn / Feather Wand / Dance Mode
programme.desc: While on a trip to the store, Bluey and Bingo create their own mini-homes side-by-side in the trolley. Later, when Bingo isn't invited to Chloe's party, she uses a magic feather wand to make things incredibly heavy.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Cousin Cody/ Hissy's Lost To
programme.desc: The pugs help their nervous puppy cousin, Cody, feel comfortable in a new place. When Hissy’s favorite toy goes missing, the pups go on a mission to find it for their kitty sister.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: The House That Bulworth Built/ Moon Rescue Mission
programme.desc: Bingo and Rolly build a brand new home for Bulworth; The pugs rescue Spot from the moon.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: I Heart Rufus/ Trolley Trouble
programme.desc: Bingo and Rolly reunite Cupcake and Rufus after a fight; Bob forgets his camera on a trolley.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Pigs and Pugs/ Bob Loves Mona
programme.desc: Two puppies who love to have fun take on adventures around their neighbourhood.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: No Bones About It/ Bob's Sock Debacle
programme.desc: When Bonnie’s dinosaur bone is lost, the pups team up with a roadrunner to find it. The pups set out to look for Bob's favorite golf socks before he participates in a tournament.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: Daddy Delivery/ Junior Flyer JP
programme.desc: While Pip and Freddy make a delivery on Father's Day, a baby Hedgehog gets stuck atop a hill and is too afraid to come down. Pip and Freddy help JP become a Super Duper Flier again after he gets demoted for failing to take a test.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: Temple of the Tiger/ The Gift-Mazing Birthday
programme.desc: Freddy and Pip try their hardest to keep a baby tiger prince clean; Freddy tries to find the perfect birthday present for one of the new babies.

programme.title: Disney Junior Ready for Preschool: Everybody Wash Your Hands
programme.desc: Doc and the toys learn how to wash their hands.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Mixed-Up Adventures: Donald's Fast Food 500/ Mini-Helpers
programme.desc: Mickey and his friends plan to surprise Donald with a waffle breakfast for his birthday. Minnie's nieces, Millie and Melody, visit the Happy Helpers office for the day.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Mixed-Up Adventures: Animal Antics/ Here, Kitty, Kitty, Kitty!
programme.desc: The gang converts their roadsters into their favorite animals; Heist's kitty runs away.

programme.title: Bluey: Backpackers / The Adventure / Copycat
programme.desc: Mum and Dad go on an adventure with the kids; Bluey and Chloe have an imaginary adventure together; Bluey decides to copy Dad.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Speedy as a Cheetah / The Soup Search
programme.desc: When Bob wishes he could run fast, the pugs find cheetahs to learn the secret of their speed. The pugs go on a journey to find the perfect bowl of soup to help Bob feel better.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Operation Fun/ Smart Attack
programme.desc: Remy Buxaplenty has come home from military school for spring break. All he can talk about is how great his new school is, making Timmy and friends jealous; Dad keeps trying to help Timmy with his homework, but he's not the brightest dad.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Cosmonopoly/ Hero Hound
programme.desc: Timmy, his dad, and fairies get stuck in a board game created by Cosmo. Timmy wishes Sparky would get a chance to be heroic.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Super Heroes/ She's Got Style
programme.desc: When Theodore thinks he's a real superhero, Alvin and Simon have a tough time convincing him he's not. Brittany is embarrassed by Jeanette's lack of style until Jeanette is proclaimed a fashion trendsetter by Brittany's icon.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Kiss Conspiracy/ House Guest
programme.desc: Simon is too embarrassed to kiss his co-star in the school play. So in a moment of desperation, he agrees to allow Alvin to switch identities with him. Alvin secretly decides to rent out their house for a Hawaiian home-swap.

programme.title: Movie: Rufus
programme.desc: After Manny makes a wish on a magic amulet, his dog Rufus turns into a real boy, and the two rule the school.

programme.title: Movie: Rufus 2
programme.desc: Rufus gets a crush on a new girl at school with a secret, but Manny and Paige become suspicious of her. A mysterious villain seeks out the magic amulet.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Boating Buddies/ Krabby Kronicle
programme.desc: Squidward is forced to attend Boating School with SpongeBob.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Slumber Party/ Grooming Gary
programme.desc: Mr. Krab's enlists SpongeBob's help to be an undercover spy at Pearl's slumber party.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Just One Bite/ The Bully
programme.desc: SpongeBob becomes obsessed with the idea that Squid must try a Krabby Patty.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Mermaidman and Barnacle Boy/ Pickles
programme.desc: Bob and Pat's favourite superheroes are in a retirement home; Bob loses self-confidence.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: As Seen on TV/ Can You Spare a Dime
programme.desc: Krabs makes a low-budget commercial; accused of stealing, Squidward quits his job.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Krab Borg/ Rock a Bye Bi-Valve
programme.desc: SpongeBob becomes paranoid; Patrick finds a baby clam and decides to rear it as his own.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: SpongeBob's House Party
programme.desc: While grocery shopping, SpongeBob discovers a 'How to Throw a Party' kit.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: New Student Starfish/ Clams
programme.desc: Pat joins SpongeBob at boating school; Mr. Krabs takes Bob and Squid fishing.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Great Snail Race/ Mid-Life Crustacean
programme.desc: Squidward races a purebred snail; Mr. Krabs follows Pat and Bob out on the town.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Krabby Land/ The Camping Episode
programme.desc: Mr. Krabs builds a children's theme park; Pat and SpongeBob camp in the backyard.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Lost Episode
programme.desc: Spongebob is jellyfishing when he realizes that he wants to fly with the jellyfish.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Fear of a Krabby Patty/ Shell of a Man
programme.desc: SpongeBob develops a fear of Krabby Patties; Mr. Krab molts his shell before a reunion.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Selling Out/ Funny Pants
programme.desc: Mr. Krabs sells his restaurant to a big company, but he soon finds retirement boring.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Squeaky Boots
programme.desc: Mr. Krabs gives SpongeBob a pair of squeaky boots in lieu of pay. No problem, as SpongeBob loves them and learns to master the squeaking noise to a new height of communication.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Krusty Towers/ Mrs. Puff, You're Fired
programme.desc: Mr. Krabs turns the Krusty Krabs into Krusty Towers, an upscale hotel where the customer is king, even the most obnoxious and demanding ones. This pushes Squidward to the point of quitting and returning as a guest to make a series of outrageous requests.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Whale of a Birthday/ Karate Island
programme.desc: Mr. Krabs wants to give his daughter the best birthday ever. SpongeBob is assigned the task of finding out what she would like; SpongeBob is invited to a karate competition on the mysterious Karate Island.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Driven to Tears/ Rule of Dumb
programme.desc: Sponge Bob fails his driver's test again, Pat asks them to prove the test can be passed.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Pink Purloiner/ Squid Wood
programme.desc: Rejected by Squidward, SpongeBob decides to make his own Squid ventriloquist dummy.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Friend or Foe
programme.desc: Mr. Krabs and Plankton have been business rivals and bitter enemies for as long as anyone can remember, but Mr. Krabs explains that this wasn't always the case. Flashbacks reveal how their friendship went sour.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: New Digs/ Krabs a la Mode
programme.desc: SpongeBob moves into the Krusty Krab and turns it into an ice skating rink.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Monkey Brains
programme.desc: Donatello and April uncover a conspiracy involving a psychic monkey.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Never Say Xever
programme.desc: Leo fears his act of mercy has come back to haunt him when Xever holds a local noodle shop owner hostage.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: The Gauntlet
programme.desc: April gets a warning from her imprisoned father, and she and the turtles go on a mission to stop Kraang from detonating a mutagen bomb on the city.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Panic in the Sewers
programme.desc: The Turtles must defend their home in the sewers when Bradford tries to destroy it with a dangerous chemical.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Mousers Attack!
programme.desc: The Turtles are split up during simultaneous threats from the Purple Dragons and Baxter Stockman's mechanical Mousers. Donnie and Mikey must prove that their 'B Team' is just as valuable as Leo and Ralph's 'A Team'.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: It Came From the Depths
programme.desc: An unstable mutant alligator possesses a strange piece of Kraang technology. While Michelangelo befriends him, the other Turtles aren't sure whether Leatherhead can be trusted or not.

programme.title: LEGO City Adventures: Buster/ Shirley Keeper
programme.desc: Firefighter Buster Lloyd gets the opportunity to live out his fantasy of being a police officer. Sanitation worker, Shirley Keeper, educates local residents on how to take care of their city.

programme.title: LEGO City Adventures: 3, 2, 1/ Paradoors
programme.desc: It's the day of the big rocket launch, and the entire city has Mars launch fever. Astronauts reach Mars orbit and prepare to launch their probes, but Tippy Doorman has other plans.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Adventures in Babysitting/ Alvin's Assistant
programme.desc: Theodore gets roped into doing Alvin's babysitting job with a spoiled four year old. Kevin agrees to be Alvin's assistant to get closer to the music industry, but ends up running errands instead.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Simon Sleeps/ Fireworks School
programme.desc: Superstars Alvin, Simon and Theodore take a roller coaster ride of music and laughter.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Simon/ Canary
programme.desc: Superstars Alvin, Simon and Theodore take a roller coaster ride of music and laughter.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Munk vs. Machine/ Shack Magic
programme.desc: The Chipmunks and Chipettes compete in a dodgeball tournament but no-one expects Theo to be their secret weapon.The Chipmunks find a shack that magically fills with toys and games and wonder if it's magic or something more sinister.

programme.title: Henry Danger: Opposite Universe
programme.desc: Henry and Charlotte are transported into an opposite universe where Ray and Schwoz are evil and want to destroy Piper, and they must figure out how to stop them, and get back to their universe.

programme.title: Henry Danger: Danger and Thunder
programme.desc: Captain Man, Kid Danger and Phoebe Thunderman infiltrate a secret villain meeting in Swellview where the villains plan to destroy Captain Man and Kid Danger.

programme.title: Henry Danger: Twin Henrys
programme.desc: Henry refuses to miss an MMA fight, so sends an android in his place to a family dinner.

programme.title: The Thundermans: Mad Max: Beyond Thunderhome
programme.desc: Max finally declares his independence from his parents' house rules and moves to his friend's house, where they only rule is that there are no rules. However, ultimate freedom quickly becomes more than Max can handle.

programme.title: The Thundermans: The Thundreth
programme.desc: Nearing their 100th save, Phoebe and Max are determined to make it their best and most memorable one yet. But by ignoring a series of small saves they overlook a super villain plotting revenge on his old rivals Hank and Barb.

programme.title: The Thundermans: Can't Hardly Date
programme.desc: As Sarah and Gideon's crushes on Max and Phoebe escalate, the twins set them up on a date with each other to solve the problem once and for all.

programme.title: The Thundermans: Make It Pop Pop
programme.desc: Nana and Pop Pop Thunderman come to visit, but when Phoebe reveals Colosso is living in their house, Pop Pop's no-tolerance attitude toward supervillains leads him to banish Max and his bunny best friend to the Detention Zone.

programme.title: The Adventures of Kid Danger: The Wahoo Punch Bro/ Pink Rocket
programme.desc: When the Wahoo Punch Bro is running amok through Swellview, Captain Man and Kid Danger must find a way to stop him. When Mitch Bilsky mocks Henry's kiddie motor scooter, Ray sets up a race between Henry and Mitch to prove whose transportation is faster.

programme.title: The Adventures of Kid Danger: Popcorn Monster/ Game of Drones
programme.desc: While trying to create two identical pieces of popcorn, Ray and Henry accidentally bring to life the Popcorn Monster. To help Ray win the Swellview Drone Dash against Dr. Minyak, Schwoz hatches a plan: shrink Kid Danger and have him pilot his own drone.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Photo Op/ Remy Rescue
programme.desc: Bill promises his family a trip to the mall food court but tricks them into going to a family portrait studio. Remy skips his violin lesson to hang out with Cricket.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Big Deal/ Forbidden Feline
programme.desc: Cricket persuades the family to hit up a Thanksgiving sale for a sweet deal on a new TV. Tilly hits it off with Anoosh, the neighborhood cat.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Reckoning Ball/ Clubbed
programme.desc: Chip Whistler returns to take revenge on the Greens, but is forced to apologize. Andromeda and Tilly follow Gloria to a club where they must 'cure' her of her amnesia.

programme.title: Amphibia: The Big Bugball Game/ Combat Camp
programme.desc: Anne must learn the value of teamwork to win the town’s annual Bugball game.

programme.title: The Owl House: Covention
programme.desc: Luz learns about witch covens, but her lesson goes awry when she finds herself thrust into a witch's duel!

programme.desc: Donald Duck and his nephews are reunited with Scrooge after moving into McDuck Manor.

programme.desc: The kids put on a concert in the backyard featuring their parents' favourite old band when Dad forgets his anniversary with Mom.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: The Chronicles of Meap
programme.desc: Space pod crashes carrying an unbearable cute alien, Meap; another adventure begins.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Meapless in Seattle
programme.desc: Meap returns to save Phineas and Ferb from his nemesis Mitch, whose real mission is to take control of all of alien-kind, putting Meap's world in danger.

programme.title: Run: Run
programme.desc: On a regular Tuesday morning, Ruby Richardson is shaken out of her humdrum suburban life when she receives an urgent text that prompts her to fly to New York and board a cross-country train, where she reunites with college ex Billy Johnson.

programme.title: Run: Kiss
programme.desc: During their first night aboard, Ruby's mind is consumed by sex, while Billy probes for personal details she's reluctant to give up. Determined to make Billy jealous, Ruby brazenly connects with Derek, a stranger on the train.

programme.title: Run: F*ck
programme.desc: Ruby and Billy decide to spend a day in Chicago before making any rash decisions about their next moves. As the two anxiously await check-in at a lavish hotel, Ruby meets an exciting woman at a department store.

programme.title: Run: Chase
programme.desc: Back on the train, Ruby seeks guidance from a new friend and gets a wakeup call from home that leaves her shaken. Meanwhile, Billy continues to lie about Fiona, who raises the stakes to get what she wants.

programme.title: Run: Jump
programme.desc: A dramatic detour finds Ruby and Billy in unknown territory as they're forced to make a life-changing decision. As Ruby tries to keep everything together and get back on course, she and an emotional Billy seek the help of an amiable local taxidermist.

programme.title: Run: Tell
programme.desc: Stranded in the middle of nowhere, Ruby and Billy argue about what to do next. Each affected by the other's desperation, the two must come together to confront their actions.

programme.title: Run: Trick
programme.desc: With the end of their journey approaching, Ruby and Billy consider their future together.

programme.title: Movie: Obsession: Stalked by My Lover
programme.desc: A young woman falls for her attractive and charismatic new roommate, but soon discovers he isn't who he says he is.

programme.desc: This is the shocking and gruesome mystery of John Wayne Gacy, a popular Chicago businessman and part-time clown who was leading a double life in the 1970s. By day he was a community leader and by night a predator of young men.

programme.title: Very Scary People: Charles Manson: The Devil's Work
programme.desc: This episode details the horror of Charles Manson's vicious attacks and explains how he was able to convince his hippie followers to brutally murder innocent strangers.

programme.title: Very Scary People: Jim Jones: Unholy Massacre
programme.desc: More than 900 Americans died in an isolated jungle in northern Guyana in November 1978. Jim Jones, has gone down in history as a madman who drove his followers to suicide. But the truth is more complicated and more tragic than that.

programme.title: Very Scary People: NorCal Rapist: Phantom Predator
programme.desc: Wanted for multiple sex attacks against women in Northern California over three decades, this masked predator meticulously planned his assaults by studying the habits and routines of his female prey. After 27 years investigators finally close in.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Whodunit
programme.desc: An evening out to a murder mystery theatre turns into a real life whodunit.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Horse Play
programme.desc: A young, happily married woman died tragically in a horseback riding accident. Her husband seemed far from grief-stricken and, when they investigated his background, police discovered a web of lies and a proficiency with pharmaceuticals.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Micro-Clues
programme.desc: When a boy is found dead in a cornfield, traces of water from his body help lead investigators to the boy's killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Deadly Parasites
programme.desc: In the spring of 1993, an unexplained illness struck the residents of Milwaukee, Wisconsin. Health officials suspected it was influenza, but it proved to be more serious and more difficult to identify.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Line of Fire
programme.desc: A fundamentalist group starts attacking and robbing banks in the Pacific Northwest.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Bad Blood
programme.desc: A young doctor in a rural community is accused of sedating and sexually assaulting two of his female patients. DNA tests demonstrate that the doctor is innocent, but the women continue to insist that he sedated and raped them.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The Talking Skull
programme.desc: A mapmaker was surveying the grounds of a boy scout ranch when something caught his eye. At first, it appeared to be a turtle shell, but it proved to be a human skull.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Deadly Delivery
programme.desc: A serial bomber was waging a vendetta against the legal profession: A judge, two attorneys and even a courthouse had been targeted. Investigators used evidence from the bombs to lead them to the perpetrator.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Where the Blood Drops
programme.desc: This is the story of a woman convicted of her husband's murder, whose son was so convinced of her innocence that he enrolled in law school.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Punch Line
programme.desc: A patient in a hospital emergency room says he cut his hand on a dumpster, but the doctor recognizes the injury as something else. That doctor became the chief witness in a crime involving the murder of a 67-year-old grandmother.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Beaten by a Hair
programme.desc: Laura Houghteling left her Bethesda home and walked to the station to take a train to work. A peculiar strand of hair found in Laura's hairbrush enabled investigators to unravel the mystery of her disappearance.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Crime Seen
programme.desc: After years in prison, Edward Honaker finally found someone who believed him, and was willing to pay for DNA testing which could prove he was innocent.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Grave Evidence
programme.desc: The shooting of a man during a hunting trip is reopened years after the incident, and the victim's hunting partner becomes a suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Deadly Formula
programme.desc: The mother of a suspiciously murdered four-month-old was convicted, but an explanation for the death would come five months later from the most unlikely of sources: his newborn brother.

programme.desc: News of the day mixed with lifestyle segments, plus weather, sports and enlightening guests.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Grounds for Indictment
programme.desc: When police have trouble pinning a suspect to the scene of the crime in a deadly drive-by-shooting, even after investigating cell phone calls and shell casings, they turn to the dirt in the perpetrator's tires.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Paintball
programme.desc: In 1962, the people of the small town of Hanford, California lost their sense of peace when one of their own, 15 year-old Marlene Miller, was murdered. It would take 24 years to bring her killer to justice.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Dressed to Kill
programme.desc: When a six-year-old girl disappears without a trace, the parents become prime suspects.

programme.title: Forensic Files: A Wrong Foot
programme.desc: Illinois police discover a footprint on a birthday card that could lead to the home invader who killed a man and attacked two young girls.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Telltale Tracks
programme.desc: Police found an abandoned car on the highway and assumed the driver had been the victim of a car-jacking. When they find the victim's body, unusual marks on her body lead to her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Material Witness
programme.desc: A teenager goes missing after an evening of horseback riding, and her body is found a month later, three miles from her home.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Order Up
programme.desc: The murder of a prominent businessman in Colorado is particularly difficult to solve since the killers left virtually no forensic evidence at the scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files: A Bag of Evidence
programme.desc: When a 9-year old girl disappears, an anonymous caller leads police to her body.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Frozen in Time
programme.desc: A young women went missing for three years until someone called to report a suspicious truck in a driveway. The cold trail turned even colder when they found out what was being stored in the truck.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Walking Terror
programme.desc: A neighbour witnesses a man stab and drown his wife. When the man is questioned by police, he remembers nothing, and is shocked at his own actions. The jury must decide if he is a murderer, or suffering from a sleep disorder.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Concrete Finisher
programme.desc: Mike heads to Moss Landing, California to learn the art of making decorative concrete countertops and fire pits. He then recounts the scariest jobs he has ever done in his career.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Bug Detective
programme.desc: Mike joins the team at Purdue University's DECOMP Forensic Entomology Lab as they dig through pig carcasses searching for maggots and other insects.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Tower Top Hand
programme.desc: Mike help erect a radio tower in North Dakota. Mike is commemorated in Hollywood for doing a dirty job in each of the 50 states.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Welcome to Dirty Jobs
programme.desc: These people tackle dirt, grime and some other not-so-nice substances; visit a cave where bat guano is collected for some of the best fertilizer in the world, and discover the dirty secret behind major league baseballs.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Big Animal Vet
programme.desc: Mike joins forces with a big animal veterinarian in Montana that has the dirty job of caring for sick farm animals, jumping into the role of apprentice veterinarian as he learns how to take care of a lama with an abscessed tooth.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Cave Biologist
programme.desc: Mike Rowe works with a cave biologist and then braves the high seas in search of slime eels.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Cranberry Farmer
programme.desc: Mike Rowe gets dirty making hand made bricks in South Carolina and then masters the art of cranberry farming in Oregon.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Welcome to Dirty Jobs
programme.desc: These people tackle dirt, grime and some other not-so-nice substances; visit a cave where bat guano is collected for some of the best fertilizer in the world, and discover the dirty secret behind major league baseballs.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Pig Farmer
programme.desc: Mike Rowe faces the challenges of feeding and cleaning up after hundreds of dirty pigs. Then, he learns how to care for a new litter of piglets, and artificially inseminates a female pig.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Chick Sexer
programme.desc: At the Murray McMurray Hatchery, Mike Rowe's job is to sort female and male chicks.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Bio-Diesel-Man
programme.desc: Join Mike Rowe as he steps into the shoes of a cobb homebuilder before teaming up with the Bee Man to cleans a church of a swarm of devilish bees. Mike also recovers grease from a restaurant and attempts to convert it into a gasoline alternative.

programme.title: Bermuda Triangle Exposed
programme.desc: Examine what's beneath the surface of the Bermuda Triangle's 440,000 square miles of ocean. The Triangle's freak waves, magnetic anomalies and giant whirlpools have been blamed for mysterious disappearances for decades.

programme.title: Captain Philips: Somali Pirates Inside Story
programme.desc: Told through eyewitness accounts, exclusive Navy footage and hijacked with extra facts, this is the story of Captain Phillips and crew aboard the Maersk Alabama and their dramatic rescue after being taken hostage by Somalian Pirates.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Deep: US Navy vs. UFO
programme.desc: Newly declassified files reveal the details of an unexplained encounter between the US Navy and UFOs that seem to defy the laws of physics. Using cutting-edge tech, Jeremy Wade investigates the truth behind this alien mystery.

programme.title: Local Knowledge: Cabin Fever
programme.desc: Ali and Rush each grab a friend and head to the water to relieve cabin fever of the pandemic lockdown.

programme.title: Legendary Locations: Places of Power
programme.desc: Josh explores three iconic monuments, including the majestic Statue of Liberty and one of the world's greatest architectural masterpieces: the Blue Mosque.

programme.title: Deadliest Catch: Dutch Harbor Double Cross
programme.desc: On the Summer Bay there's hell to pay when Bill's backup block goes missing. Cornelia Marie skippers Josh and Casey navigate a tenuous partnership at the Russian line. Weighed down by three full tanks, Sig puts a heavy Northwestern in Mandy's hands.

programme.title: Deadliest Catch: Bomb Cyclone
programme.desc: The Wizard catches fire in a violent storm known as a bomb cyclone. Josh and Casey attempt to fish in 30-foot seas, while two rogue waves slam the Southern Wind. Wild Bill grinds his crew 72 straight hours bringing himself to the brink of collapse.

programme.title: Dino Hunters: Curse of the Carnivore
programme.desc: Clayton Phipps and his team prepare to flip a 20,000-pound carnivore fossil out of the earth and onto a flatbed to get it to the lab for analysis. Meanwhile, Mike Harris hunts for a duckbill skull and Jared Hudson uncovers an ancient reptile.

programme.title: Dino Hunters: Shots Fired
programme.desc: Clayton must haul his 20,000-pound dinosaur fossil over 200 miles to a lab where it will finally be identified. The Harris/Bolan clan chases poachers off of their land and Jared makes a last-ditch effort to bring in some money this season.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid: Edge of Madness
programme.desc: Two survivalists take on the hellish conditions of Guyana's equatorial savannah. Slammed from day one by the heat, they battle debilitating health issues, a team dynamic breakdown, critical survival malfunctions and a massive psychological decline.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid: Surthrive
programme.desc: An independent mother of three, plus a jungle savvy Green Beret, take on the remote wetlands of Guyana. Battling disease-carrying sand flies, venomous snakes, and an infestation of caiman present huge challenges.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid: Blood in the Water
programme.desc: Two survivalists test their primitive skills on the razor sharp terrain of Andros Island in the Bahamas. Battling starvation, swarms of tiny predators and accusations about a partner's survival abilities turns this partnership into a bad marriage.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid: Thieves in the Night
programme.desc: One survivalist falls into a roaring fire after weeks of depleted energy due to no food. With just days to go, the pair makes a daring plan to survive.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid XL: No Calm After the Storm
programme.desc: A brutal windstorm punishes the survivalists. A falling out with Bulent leads Makani to banish herself and risk going it alone. Kate finds a new group, and the desperate team goes on a hunt that could make or break their challenge.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: Fashionably Late

programme.desc: Mike's house is so toxic that a virus she contracted in the home caused her kidneys to fail, nearly killing her. Bonnie's home is hoarded and in need of repair.

programme.title: Hoarders: Sandy and Len
programme.desc: Len lives in a home stuffed full of garbage bags where he keeps all his belongings. If he doesn't clean up, his family fears his house will kill him.

programme.title: Hoarders: Phyllis/ Janet
programme.desc: Phyllis' house is packed with a floor to ceiling collection of bags stuffed with dolls. Janet has to crawl over mountains of garbage to get to the one recliner where she eats and sleeps.

programme.title: Hoarders: Linda
programme.desc: Linda hadn't allowed her family into her home for years. A recent medical emergency exposed the secret she'd been hiding. What her daughter found in her home shocked her and made her question her mother's sanity.

programme.title: Hoarders: Dorothy and David and Doris
programme.desc: Dorothy and David fell in love with each other over their mutual love of hoarding. Their home is so packed now it has become physically unsafe for Dorothy, and doctors have said one more fall in the hoard could paralyze her.

programme.title: The First 48: Ditched/ Misstep
programme.desc: In Birmingham, motorists discover a decomposed body on the side of the interstate. In Charlotte, detectives try to find the killers of a man shot behind his girlfriend's house.

programme.title: The First 48: Flashing Colors
programme.desc: In Gwinnett County, a teenager is gunned down outside a nightclub when he winds up at the center of a gang feud.

programme.title: The First 48: In a Lonely Place
programme.desc: In New Orleans, a young mother is found shot and burned in the trunk of her car when a night out on the town becomes her last.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Remember the Pact
programme.desc: Find out how many mousetraps accidentally went off during Q's turn in 'hold this please,' what made Joe and Murr join 'team skins' in Columbus park, and how Murr's tech punishment made show history.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Lost Boy
programme.desc: Find out which joker once worked as a fish monger, who lied on his resume to snag a TV internship and what chain restaurant the guys used during Sal's 'shush!' punishment at the mall.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Take Me Out at the Ballgame
programme.desc: Find out how Murr chose which guys to honk at Volcano Bay, what happened to the TP used in the stock boy shootout, and how Joe stayed safe after messing with NY Mets fans at Citi Field.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Just Say No
programme.desc: Find out which joker loves blackjack, what baseball team two of the guys split season tickets for and how they found the lovely lady whose heart Q broke during his ball field punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Dinner Party Show 7
programme.desc: Four friends compete to embarrass each other in the ultimate hidden camera showdown.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Dover and Out
programme.desc: The guys survey employees with odd questions, and tonight's loser unveils a mural in a small town whose residents react with big city rage.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Party Crasher
programme.desc: Find out which jokers were in on the surprise Gary Busey appearance, where the phrase 'tit for tat' came from, and how Murr's big mouth backfired on him, leading to this Statue of Liberty punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Putting the P in Pool
programme.desc: Find what Sal finds stressful about posing as a cashier; what's Q's favorite on-set beverage; and when did the crew have to arrive poolside to get a front row seat for Joe's pee punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Sneaking Number Twos, Going Number One
programme.desc: Find out why the jokers love filming at the grocery store, how many pencils were purchased for put-pocketing and why Sal's pee punishment was twice as terrible as it looked.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Car Sick
programme.desc: Find out what celebrity Sal wrote fan letters to as a kid, which grocery game is Murr's favourite, how the guys found sloppy Joe and who helped Q's first jeep punishment a milky mess.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Dinner Party Show 7
programme.desc: Four friends compete to embarrass each other in the ultimate hidden camera showdown.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: No Good Deed
programme.desc: Find out what after-school activity Joe's mom forbid him from doing, why filming nearly halted during the jack asses challenge and what last minute idea made it into Sal and Joe's charity funds punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Indecent Proposal
programme.desc: Find out why Murr had to shave in under three minutes between scenes of That Ain't Me, what the Jokers love most about Writers Blocked, and where Sal ranks his indecent dance instructor punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Lords of the Ring
programme.desc: Find out which joker always brings a bag of dental hygiene products to set, why Q changed into cargo pants in a store window and where the art department found the turquoise ring.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Dinner Party Show 7
programme.desc: Four friends compete to embarrass each other in the ultimate hidden camera showdown.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Pulling the Rug
programme.desc: Find out where Joe originally wanted to play hide n' seek, what Q's favourite dessert is, and what the crew was most afraid of during Murr's wig-pulling punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Running of the Bullies
programme.desc: Find out which Joker's waiter experience made meal or no meal a breeze, why there was a white noise machine needed during dust bunnies and who issued a siren warning right after Sal's pool punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Needy and the Greedy
programme.desc: A behind the scenes look at the location that refused to let the Jokers film, Joe's injury from being thrown into a tower of cereal and why Q was called a 'loose cannon'.

programme.title: Comedy Knockout: Show Some Respect
programme.desc: Dave Hill confesses who he has on the brain, J.P. McDale tales a shot at Dave's career, and Ali Kolbert reveals her side hustle.

programme.title: Comedy Knockout: Made Me Laugh
programme.desc: Vanessa Fraction spots a 70's sitcom star in the crowd, Rojo Perez makes an inappropriate exit, and Tone Bell reads a crowd member's mind.

programme.title: Comedy Knockout: I Hate Myself
programme.desc: On this show, we learn three things: don't let Mo Amer call 911, don't ask Jenny Zigrino how you look, and never let Mo Mandel speak to the audience.

programme.title: Comedy Knockout: Let the Record Show
programme.desc: Adam Ray steps out of a time machine, Brad Williams almost makes his head explode in a rant about airplanes, and Sarah Tiana reveals how Brad gets a waiter's attention.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Thrillseekers 2
programme.desc: Stars offer hilarious commentaries while viewing footage of the world's dumbest people.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Performer 11
programme.desc: A rapper whose balance is worse than his rhymes, a flaming embarrassment of a TV chef, a dancer who lost her sexy a long time ago and a weather man with serious arachnophobia.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Starfart Macchiato
programme.desc: Find out what game the Jokers have played five times throughout the show's history, and how the crew got poison ivy. Plus, learn what game the Jokers played that had them asking for extra turns.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Bellydancer
programme.desc: Learn about Murr and Sal's favourite sandwich, which Joker is certified in CPR and what gift Murr received when the show first started.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Centaur of Attention
programme.desc: The guys go head-to-head in a focus group, and then spread some confusion at a bagel store before heading to the park where they hit all the wrong notes. Finally, tonight's biggest loser gets hunted down during a truly humiliating walk of shame.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Scarytales
programme.desc: The guys are forced to look for love in all the wrong places at a singles' event, and then phone it in making embarrassing calls on strangers' cells.

programme.title: Movie: Evan Almighty
programme.desc: Evan Baxter, a Congressman looking to change the world, is told by God to build an ark before the flood arrives.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Performers 12
programme.desc: Butter-fingered figure-skaters, busted-ass break dancers, klutzy beauty pageant contestants and comedians who are only funny because they're so not funny.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: Performers 13
programme.desc: Counting down 20 of the dumbest performers you've ever seen including a woman dancing in butter and team mascots gone wild.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Partiers 5
programme.desc: Be a witness to 20 all-new party moments that you have to see to believe! Grandma's doing keg stands, karaoke singers unable to sing their ABCs, and Aussies shooting fireworks from down under are just a few of the wild moments in this episode.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Partiers 6
programme.desc: A naked john runs after the prostitute who stole all his clothes, with memories that will linger after the hangover has passed.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Partiers 7
programme.desc: In an ongoing mission to bring bad behavior to light, The Smoking Gun presents 20 of the World's Dumbest Partiers. This week, an upside-down drinking contest leaves everyone doused with beer. Meanwhile, a drunk Russian woman does an impromptu strip tease.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Mime and Punishment
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Murr and Q put the nasty in namaste when they lead guided meditations. Tonight's big loser gets boxed in with a 24-hour punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Three Men and Your Baby
programme.desc: Sal, Joe, Murr, and Q get snap happy posing as photographers, and all but one of the guys are faced with a punishment that's not child's play.

programme.title: At Home With Amy Sedaris: Dips and Dunkers
programme.desc: Amy hosts a party to watch the big game in hopes of attracting a younger TV watching demographic. A special guest shows her how to prepare the perfect game day dip.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Rubbed the Wrong Way
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Q and Murr cruise the food court sending texts to strangers, then throw caution, and groceries, to the wind. And tonight's loser is cast as a genie in a play but wishes he could be anywhere else.

programme.title: Movie: Home Again
programme.desc: A group of people are deported to Jamaica after growing up in Toronto, New York and London.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: DDD Family Redemption
programme.desc: DDD Family Tournament teams who went home early are getting a duo do-over. First, Guy Fieri challenges the chefs to flip coffee and doughnuts into dinner. Then he gives the remaining DDD family duos a $22 budget to make their surf and turf dishes.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Flavortown International
programme.desc: Guy goes global with flavours from all over the world. In Warr Acres, Oklahoma, a Middle Eastern smorgasbord serves standout samosas and chicken kebab. A funky spot in Portland, Oregon turns out real-deal Mexican tortas.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner
programme.desc: Guy Fieri travels to Tampa Bay for authentic Mexican street food served out of a bus. In Virginia Beach, he has dinner at a popular seafood joint, and in San Antonio he visits a breakfast joint.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Bar Food and Bon Bons
programme.desc: Guy rolls coast to coast looking for flavour, and finds it in the most interesting places.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Sandwiches, Southern and South of the Border
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri's diving into sandwiches, Southern eats and south-of-the-border specialties.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Soakin' Up Maui
programme.desc: Guy's cruising the islands with his buddy, Chef Reno Henriques, on a culinary tour of Maui, Hawaii.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Multitaskers
programme.desc: A Greek diner is decked out in neon and servin' up family recipes like Yia Yia's grape leaves. In Oxnard, Calif., the jack of all trades joint is baking, brewing and cooking all in the same restaurant.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Fresh Fish and Funky Chicken
programme.desc: Guy is hitting the road for some outrageous seafood and tasty chicken. In Des Moines, Iowa, the trophy-winning barbecue joint is dishing out unique wings and an apple dumpling dessert.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Name That Kitchen
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's checkin' out some righteous spots that say a lot in the name. In Fort Collins, Colo., an NFL pro and Triple-D alum team up on gangster green chili and mile-high burgers.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Savory with a Side of Sweet
programme.desc: On this trip, Guy Fieri is jumping into some sweet and savory dishes. In Burlington, Vt., a vegan joint gettin' crazy with a crunch wrap and coconut-banana French toast.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Real Deal Fast Food
programme.desc: Guy tracks down some great places that prove fast food can also be great food.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Totally Fried
programme.desc: Guy Fieri searches for great joints where they're frying up a storm.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Sweet 'n Savory
programme.desc: Guy discovers homemade banana cream pie in Minnesota.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Old Standards, New Styles
programme.desc: This trip, Guy is hitting up spots serving comfort food that has been turned up a notch. In Long Beach, CA., and old school diner with a new chef is cooking some home-style classics.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: One Street Wonders
programme.desc: This trip, Guy is heading to Cincinnati to try three righteous restaurants all on the same street. He checks out The Funky Pub where they're serving up all kinds of hot dogs and sammies.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: You Can Only Get It Here
programme.desc: Guy is on the hunt for funky flavours, starting in San Francisco at an Asian/American/Mexican fusion joint.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Globe Tasting
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's getting an authentic taste of all ends of the earth. In Bakersfield, Calif., the firehouse-turned-restaurant firing up Latin-fusion favorites like basque lamb tacos and popcorn pork with a side of zing.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: From Biscuits to Brisket
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri's digging in to stick-to-your-ribs comfort food. In Atlanta, the down-home joint cooking up righteous chicken and biscuits and house-cured lox.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Legacies
programme.desc: Guy Fieri looks into some classic restaurant favourites in America.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Cookin' It Old School
programme.desc: Guy Fieri uncovers three joints putting' a piece of history on the table: In New Orleans, an oyster bar where nothing's changed since 1919 and the deep fried oysters are still hand-shucked.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Like Mama Made
programme.desc: Guy travels to St. Louis where a former backup singer is serving old school soul food.

programme.title: Giada at Home

programme.title: Brunch @ Bobby's: Buttermilk Brunch
programme.desc: Chef Bobby Flay is breaking out the buttermilk, his secret weapon for brunch! First up, golden raisin-fennel soda bread with orange-buttermilk butter, followed by tender buttermilk waffles topped with golden buttermilk fried chicken.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back to Basics: Cook Like A Pro: Salads For Four Seasons
programme.desc: Ina Garten is sharing her pro tips for sensational salads for every season. For an all-in-one spring dinner, her crispy mustard chicken and frisee salad is unbeatable, and tarragon shrimp salad is perfect for an easy and delicious summer lunch.

programme.title: Valerie's Home Cooking: Surf's Up
programme.desc: Valerie Bertinelli has always loved vacationing in Hawaii, where she enjoys the food, relaxed atmosphere and opportunities to surf. She's bringing the aloha vibe home with a Hawaiian-inspired feast for her husband.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Make Ahead Marvels
programme.desc: It's make-ahead marvels all the way on the Drummond ranch.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Breakfast, Lunch and Cowboys
programme.desc: Ree's doing some cowgirl as she pitches in with the family and cowboys working calves. She makes them a breakfast of apple fritters with bacon and sausage, followed by a lunch of pulled pork, coleslaw, and baked beans.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Shakespeare Movie Night
programme.desc: Ree's hosting a movie night for her daughter's classmates and preparing a Roman-inspired menu to accompany it.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Home Sweet Home 8
programme.desc: Ree has a day of cooking, cattle working and a real cowboy lunch at the ranch, shot by her kid crew. Ree makes crispy breaded easy pork chops, amazing smashed potatoes and baked beans with peppers.

programme.title: Girl Meets Farm: Brunch All Day
programme.desc: Molly is having a brunch party with a few of her former bridesmaids. She makes blueberry blintzes, a breakfast salad with soft cooked eggs and crispy walnut sausage patties.

programme.title: Valerie's Home Cooking: Lights, Camera ... Eat
programme.desc: Valerie Bertinelli is developing new, foolproof recipes for her website. She makes a batch of savory breakfast muffins and kale caesar salad with crispy garlic and oregano topping along with pork tenderloin steaks with mustard herb sauce.

programme.title: Delicious Miss Brown: Lowcountry Classics
programme.desc: Kardea Brown shares classic dishes inspired by her heritage and the place she calls home, South Carolina's Lowcountry. Her Southern staples include Gullah-style shrimp and grits, Gullah red rice, Gullah Caviar and classic Charleston chewie’s.

programme.title: Symon's Dinners Cooking Out: Two Grills and One Mother-In-Law Inspired Meal
programme.desc: Michael Symon creates creamy chicken and dumplings on the grill. Michael's wife makes drop biscuits to serve as the dumplings while Michael picks fresh herbs for the filling. Finally, Michael makes bananas foster on the grill.

programme.title: The Kitchen: Family Style: Can You Grill It?
programme.desc: The hosts pull out unexpected and flavourful foods to cook on the grill all summer long. Sunny starts with a grilled open-faced BLT and Geoffrey follows with grilled potatoes with chipotle yogurt. Then, Jeff makes a charred caprese salad.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Rockin' New Orleans
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri's hitting up funky joints in New Orleans that both locals and tourists are digging into, and they're servin' up all kinds of flavor.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: World Class Flavor
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri's crossing the country to get a taste of world-class flavour. From California to Florida and Seattle to Massachusetts, he's divin' into classic Greek, legit pierogis, and real-deal Belgian waffles made European-style.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Extra Helpings
programme.desc: This trip, it's an exclusive peek at the lost footage of DDD: the places Guy Fieri's visited before, but all-new dishes never seen until now. Guy's digging in to money mussels, crispy pork skin and a lights-out lamb sandwich.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Arguments for the Quickie
programme.desc: When Charlie finds out that Mia is in town with her dance troupe, he opts not to see her and tries to convince Alan that he's over her. Charlie gets another surprise when Mia explains that she wants something personal from him, his sperm.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: That Pistol-Packin' Hermaphrodite
programme.desc: Wedding bells loom as Charlie and Mia make their wedding plans, but when their families meet, things immediately begin to fall apart. Ultimately, Charlie and his bride-to-be end up deciding on a small ceremony in Vegas.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Who Is This Vod Kanockers?
programme.desc: Alan feels guilty when he learns that Charlie and Mia didn't tie the knot because she had wanted him and Jake to move out. Meanwhile, Charlie and his rock star neighbour don't get along.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: The Sea Is a Harsh Mistress
programme.desc: Charlie tries surfing in order to impress a beautiful girl, with disastrous results. He later tells Alan that when his life was hanging in the balance, their deceased father came to him and made a request that Charlie is reluctant to fulfill.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: A Pot-Smoking Monkey
programme.desc: Alan is determined to gain custody of his and Kandi's dog after she walks away from their split with both the condo and the car. Having also lost everything to Judith following their divorce, Alan decides to hire her lawyer.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: A Live Woman of Proven Fertility
programme.desc: Alan is overjoyed that he'll no longer owe Judith alimony when she marries Herb. When their marriage plans are threatened due to Jake's disdain for his soon-to-be-stepfather, Alan enlists Charlie's help to keep Herb from getting cold feet.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Apologies for the Frivolity
programme.desc: Though Charlie despises his sharp-tongued, passive aggressive mother, he's strangely attracted to Lydia, an attractive, blonde high-end real estate agent, just like his mother. Unfortunately, the similarities don't end there.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Repeated Blows to His Unformed Head
programme.desc: When Berta brings her pregnant daughter, the one she thought would make something of herself, to Charlie's house to help clean, Alan gets to satisfy one of his secret fetishes. Meanwhile, Charlie ventures out of his familiar Malibu surroundings.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Release the Dogs
programme.desc: Unable to sleep, Alan takes one of Charlie's suggestions and goes for a jog on the beach. When his jog takes a disastrous turn, he then turns to a psychiatrist in the hopes that discovering the source of his insomnia will help alleviate it.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Corey's Been Dead for An Hour
programme.desc: Charlie becomes annoyed with Alan for constantly sponging off him and promising to cover the tab 'next time.' When Alan tries to leave Charlie the cheque for dinner with two women, Charlie tries to beat him at his own game.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Walnuts and Demerol
programme.desc: As Charlie prepares for a steamy Christmas Eve with his date, friends and family keep dropping in and staying. When Evelyn discovers who Charlie's date is, she inexplicably becomes desperate to break them up before they go too far.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Castrating Sheep in Montana
programme.desc: Alan expects the worst when Berta learns that he's been seeing her daughter, Naomi, but Berta's reaction is surprising and much worse than Alan could have imagined.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Don't Worry, Speed Racer
programme.desc: When Jake tells Charlie and Alan that he often overhears his mother and Herb having sex, it triggers a repressed memory for Charlie. Rose tries to help Charlie work through the problem.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: That's Summer Sausage, Not Salami
programme.desc: After getting to know Danielle, Charlie discovers that she shares some common interests with Alan and proceeds to set them up, but when things begin to heat up between them, Charlie becomes jealous.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: My Damn Stalker
programme.desc: When Rose announces she is moving to London, Charlie must face his true feelings for her. Meanwhile, Alan fudges his profile on an Internet dating service, attracting a fellow profile embellisher, Beverly.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Young People Have Phlegm Too
programme.desc: A sexy young girl who Charlie has been dating cajoles him into going to a hot new club and bringing Alan along to meet her friend. When they arrive, Charlie and Alan are mortified to learn that the partygoers consider them 'old.'

programme.title: The King of Queens: Separation Anxiety
programme.desc: Doug and Carrie have different opinions about Deacon and Kelly's recent separation.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Separation Anxiety
programme.desc: Doug and Carrie have different opinions about Deacon and Kelly's recent separation.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: What Happens in Vegas Is Disgusting in Vegas
programme.desc: Typically tightened-up New Christine loses all her inhibitions at a spur-of-the-moment bachelorette party Christine and Barb throw for her in Vegas.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Reckless Abandonment
programme.desc: When Barb and Matthew both announce they're moving out and Ritchie leaves for science camp, Christine tries to prove to everyone, including herself, that she's capable of taking care of herself.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Honey, I Ran Over the Kid

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Too Close for Christine

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: A Change of Heart/ Pants
programme.desc: Christine and Richard help Marly and Lindsay through labour, evoking memories of Ritchie's birth and renewing romantic feelings.

programme.desc: Blanche tries to cheer up a lonely Dorothy by taking her out to her favourite bar.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: A Midwinter Night's Dream
programme.desc: An ancient family curse requires Sophia to save Dorothy by performing strange tasks.

programme.desc: The full moon and festivities prompt strange happenings including Blanche's jewelry being swiped, Sophia trying to break a curse, and Dorothy and Miles sharing a passionate kiss.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Home Again, Rose

programme.title: The Golden Girls: One Flew Out of the Cuckoo's Nest
programme.desc: Blanche has a date and tries to dump her visiting uncle Lucas on Dorothy. Later, to get revenge, Dorothy and Lucas play a trick on Blanche by telling her they're getting married. Rose announces she plans on moving, Lucas decides to reveal the truth.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: One Flew Out of the Cuckoo's Nest
programme.desc: Dorothy accepts Lucas's marriage proposal and they wed. Blanche, Rose, and Dorothy say a tearful goodbye with Dorothy planning on moving to Atlanta. Meanwhile, Sophia makes a final decision on where she wants to live.

programme.title: Crikey! It's the Irwins: Swimming With Steve
programme.desc: Bindi swims alongside whale sharks, assisting with research efforts on the world's latest and least understood fish. Robert work to save an echidna that's tightly trapped under a fridge. Terri joins a closeup exam on a grizzly.

programme.title: Crikey! It's the Irwins: Robert's Alligator Feeding Frenzy
programme.desc: Robert feeds 44 hungry alligators, all at the same time. Terri helps examine Kaitlynn the tiger and makes a thrilling discovery, while Bindi follows the heartbreaking story of her old friends, Zambezi the Zebra.

programme.title: Crikey! It's the Irwins: Bindi Says Yes!
programme.desc: As Bindi celebrates her 21st birthday, she gets a surprise proposal from her boyfriend, Chandler. Meanwhile, things get dangerous as Robert wrangles gators in thick mud. Terri helps with the momentous birth of a baby white rhino.

programme.title: Crikey! It's the Irwins: Bindi's Wedding
programme.desc: Bindi and Chandler prepare for the happiest day of their lives. Weeks before their wedding day, the coronavirus pandemic causes them to make difficult and drastic changes to the wedding of their dreams, but in the end love prevails.

programme.title: Crikey! It's the Irwins: Life in Lockdown
programme.desc: It's all Irwins on deck when the zoo closes due to the global pandemic. Terri must race to find funds to keep all the animals fed, while newlyweds Bindi and Chandler jump in, trading their honeymoon for hard work, and Robert faces a risky python rescue.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: Sneak Peek
programme.desc: Tia Maria Torres works to save pit bulls from dangerous and abusive situations.

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet: Pandemic Emergency
programme.desc: Dr. Jeff and his dedicated team continue their work through the chaos of the COVID-19 pandemic. A beloved dog is hit by a car and needs surgery to save its leg, and a cherished cat is rushed in with a mysterious bleed. A puppy is suspected of eating DVDs.

programme.title: Crikey! It's the Irwins: Turtles Take Flight
programme.desc: Robert and Terri take to the skies to release three rescued sea turtles near the Great Barrier Reef. Bindi anxiously awaits the birth of the zoo's first tiger cub in years. Richie helps a baby wallaby in his struggle for survival.

programme.title: Crikey! It's the Irwins: Life in Lockdown
programme.desc: It's all Irwins on deck when the zoo closes due to the global pandemic. Terri must race to find funds to keep all the animals fed, while newlyweds Bindi and Chandler jump in, trading their honeymoon for hard work, and Robert faces a risky python rescue.

programme.title: Crikey! It's the Irwins: Steve's Legacy Continues
programme.desc: After months of training, Robert steps into his father's shoes to feed Graham, Australia Zoo's most notorious crocodile, solo, in front of the entire Crocoseum. Bindi witnesses one of nature's rarest events: Cedar the Koala has given birth to twins.

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet: Pandemic Emergency
programme.desc: Dr. Jeff and his dedicated team continue their work through the chaos of the COVID-19 pandemic. A beloved dog is hit by a car and needs surgery to save its leg, and a cherished cat is rushed in with a mysterious bleed. A puppy is suspected of eating DVDs.

programme.title: Crikey! It's the Irwins: Giraffe Road Trip
programme.desc: The family prepares Croc One, Steve Irwin's research ship, for a mission to release an endangered sea turtle back to the wild. Scarlett the Giraffe is reunited with her grandmother, and Grub the wild Echidna is released from the Zoo's Wildlife Hospital.

programme.title: Crikey! It's the Irwins: Robert, King of Cobras
programme.desc: Robert follows in Steve's steps wrangling Utah, a highly venomous 12-foot king cobra. Dr. Amber visits Tandora to care for wild Koalas. Back at the Zoo, an old injury flares up for Bruce the Brolga, a bird who's been in the Irwin's care for 26 years.

programme.title: Crikey! It's the Irwins: Bindi's Lemur Island
programme.desc: The Australia Zoo's Alligator Snapping Turtles need a health check, and it's Robert's job to protect from their fierce bites. Terri and Bindi welcome seven new Lemurs from the US. The Zoo's Rescue Unit is called to save possums caught in a chimney.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Hunting Hunters
programme.desc: Across Texas, the wardens enforce the law during prime deer season. Bad weather conditions lead to a serious car wreck. A tipster reports an injured owl. A hunter harassment issue between neighbors is addressed. Illegal deer disposals are investigated.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Finding a Felon
programme.desc: In Texas, Game Wardens are the guardians of the state's wildlife. Game Warden Herchman saves an owl, while Game Wardens Richards and McGee team up to work a case in an archery-only county. Game Wardens Benge and King investigate a poaching case.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Pelicans and Poachers
programme.desc: On the Gulf, oyster boats and fish retailers are inspected. Outside San Antonio, a poacher is caught after posting pictures. In East Texas, Game Wardens patrol deer camps and help a pelican in distress.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Small Town Troubles
programme.desc: Game wardens are ready for springtime in Texas. Wardens investigate a video of an illegal hunter. A concerned citizen reports a hunter on his property. A fisherman is illegally catching oysters. A game warden smells something fishy going on.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: In Hot Water
programme.desc: Dockery responds to a call about an alligator found near a residential area. The game wardens find blood in a hunter's car after a hunter claimed he didn't shoot anything. Boone follows up on a call about five racoons making their way into someone's home.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Trashed
programme.desc: Across Texas, game wardens work day and night to enforce the law. In East Texas, a young woman is served an arrest warrant. A fisherman has no fishing license but plenty of outstanding warrants. Outside Austin, someone illegally dumps a pile of trash.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Deadly Conduct

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Midnight Manhunt

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Rattled

programme.title: The Last Dance

programme.title: The Last Dance

programme.desc: When a Kansas mother is discovered brutally murdered in her own home, investigators fear that her missing teenage daughter has met a similar fate. They scour the area for clues until rumors guide them to an unlikely mastermind.

programme.desc: When an apparent slam dunk murder investigation becomes muddied by intricate stories, police discover their star witness is much closer to the murderer than they were initially led to believe.

programme.title: Movie: Money Monster

programme.title: The Simpsons: Homer vs. Lisa and the 8th Commandment
programme.desc: Homer gets an illegal cable hook-up and Lisa refuses to watch for fear of losing her soul.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Lisa vs. Malibu Stacey
programme.desc: Lisa creates a feminist doll and goes head-to-head with Malibu Stacey.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Treehouse of Horror XIII
programme.desc: Homer discovers that his new hammock can create clones; Marge is transformed into a cat.

programme.title: The Simpsons: 'Scuse Me While I Miss the Sky
programme.desc: A British filmmaker visits Bart's class to make a documentary about an American school.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Girls Just Want to Have Sums
programme.desc: Springfield is in an uproar after Principal Skinner makes a sexist comment at the end of an Itchy and Scratchy musical. Principal Skinner is replaced by Melanie Upfoot, a women's education expert, who divides the school into two different schools.

programme.title: The Simpsons: The Burns and the Bees
programme.desc: Lisa tries to save the endangered Springfield bee population from Mr. Burns and his new sports arena. Homer attempts to help the quickly diminishing bee population by mating the Springfield bees with Africanized bees, creating a fiasco.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Loan-a-Lisa
programme.desc: Grampa gives each member of the family a portion of his savings. Lisa invests in Nelson's new business venture, but realizes that his instant success might lure him away from the classroom.

programme.title: The Simpsons: The Man Who Grew Too Much
programme.desc: On a research trip, Lisa is shocked to discover that Sideshow Bob is now the chief scientist at a massive chemical engineering company, but her fears are allayed as she becomes enamoured with his likeminded appreciation of high-culture.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Paths of Glory
programme.desc: Lisa sets out to restore the tarnished reputation of Springfield's first female inventor. To find her invention, she scours an abandoned asylum and a restaurant that caters to men.

programme.title: The Simpsons: The Last Traction Hero
programme.desc: A workplace accident leaves Homer in a cast, and in a position to sue Mr. Burns. This leaves Marge unfulfilled, so she turns to an unexpected source for romance. Meanwhile, Lisa is made 'Bus Monitor' and tries to keep the peace.

programme.title: The Blue Planet: Frozen Seas
programme.desc: A look at the survival techniques of creatures that endure the harsh conditions of the Arctic and Antarctic.

programme.title: Africa: Kalahari
programme.desc: In Africa's ancient southwest corner, two deserts sit side by side; water is in short supply, yet these deserts are full of life because the creatures that live there have turned the rules of survival on their head.

programme.title: Africa: Savannah
programme.desc: East Africa is a land that is constantly changing. In order to survive, creatures must be able to deal with twists and turns, no matter how hostile and unpredictable. This unique and varied land is a haven for life.

programme.title: Africa: Congo
programme.desc: The Congo rainforest covers the very heart of Africa and is a dynamic and pulsating habitat, packed with life. From the competitive centre of the forest to the open edge of the Atlantic Ocena, this is all about carving out space in a crowded world.

programme.title: Africa: Cape
programme.desc: Two great ocean currents, one cold and one warm, sweep around the continent's Great Cape.

programme.title: Africa: Sahara
programme.desc: North Africa is home to the greatest desert on Earth, the Sahara. This vast wilderness is the toughest part of the African continent.

programme.title: Hidden Habitats: Svalbard
programme.desc: A Look at the archipelago of Svalbard in the Arctic Circle. Polar bears, ringed seals, sea birds and reindeer all feature in the food web but the ecosystem is driven by the producers at the bottom of the food chain.

programme.title: Hidden Habitats: Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: The Great Barrier Reef's size is revealed as a mosaic of environments. However, scale isn't the only success of this micro world. The reef exists in nutrient poor waters, which on the face of it, is too poor to support all this life.

programme.title: Hidden Habitats: Australia's Red Centre
programme.desc: The Red Center of Australia is one of the most inhospitable environments on Earth yet it is filled with wildlife. The secret to Australia's diversity is its rainforest past.

programme.title: The Blue Planet: Seasonal Seas

programme.title: The Blue Planet: Coral Seas

programme.title: The Blue Planet: Tidal Seas

programme.title: The Blue Planet: Coasts
programme.desc: Coasts, the boundary between land and sea, are exciting places with animals constantly coming and going.

programme.title: The '90s: The Last Great Decade?: Shock and Awe
programme.desc: In the '90s, new attitudes and new technology equal more freedom, more fragmentation, more diversity, but also more conflict.

programme.title: The '90s: The Last Great Decade?: Exposed
programme.desc: It’s the countdown to the end of the century and the party of all parties, but pre-millennium tension is rising.

programme.title: The '90s: The Last Great Decade?: The Countdown
programme.desc: The world falls in love with a heartbroken Princess Diana and grieves her passing. Americans are shocked by the senseless killings at Columbine, and surprised by the 1998 midterm elections. 'The West Wing' reaches TV screens; Lewinsky affair continues.

programme.title: The '80s: The Decade That Made Us: Lift Off
programme.desc: See how the '80s created our modern world including the revolution in personal entertainment brought on by Pac-Man and Sony Walkmans while a new kid on the block defined a new musical era - spelled 'MTV.'

programme.title: The '80s: The Decade That Made Us: The Revolutionaries
programme.desc: Reagan's business mandate leads to a new breed of entrepreneurs, including Steve Jobs, whose '1984' ad helped launch the Super Bowl commercial craze and Ted Turner, who invented the 24-hour news network.

programme.title: The '80s: The Decade That Made Us: Shop 'Til You Drop
programme.desc: Glamour finds a new poster girl in the 1980s, when a young singer is launched to stardom with a shocking performance of 'Like a Virgin' at the first MTV Music Video Awards inspiring girls across the globe to hit the mall.

programme.title: The '80s: The Decade That Made Us: Masters of the Universe
programme.desc: With the motto 'greed is good,' Gordon Gekko desires excess and ambition in 'Wall Street' while traders mirror his bad behaviour in real life. CEOs see their salaries rise while women fight to overcome office boys' clubs.

programme.title: The '80s: The Decade That Made Us: Tear Down These Walls
programme.desc: Though the 1980s were known for its 'greed is good' mantra, the decade also saw its fair share of literal and cultural walls brought down, as Americans united on political, racial and health issues.
